# Copy mode (the default) into a sink in the same directory would leave the
# package with duplicate top-level declarations: the source keeps every
# selected decl and the sink gains a copy.
# Expect: exit 1, actionable error suggesting -move, no sink written.
cp a.go a_before.go
! exec sflit -source a.go -regex '^Filter$' -sink b.go
stderr 'cannot copy within the same directory'
stderr 'use -move'
! exists b.go
cmp a.go a_before.go

# The same invocation with -move is the supported same-directory split.
exec sflit -source a.go -regex '^Filter$' -sink b.go -move
cmp a.go expected_a.go
cmp b.go expected_b.go

-- a.go --
package probe

func Filter() {}

func Keep() {}
-- expected_a.go --
package probe

func Keep() {}
-- expected_b.go --
package probe

func Filter() {}
